title: leptinella traillii subsp. pulchella
intro: Leptinella traillii subsp. pulchella, also known as golden buttons or small daisy, is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of New Zealand and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and skin irritations. Leptinella traillii subsp. pulchella can be prepared as a tea or tincture for its health benefits, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
